body:
Before, the `c_p` and `R` in computation of maximum supersaturation in the `AerosolActivation` module were taken for the dry air. I think it's better to take them for moist air. The [original paper](https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1029/97JD03735) was a little ambiguous with regards to that for me. I think they might have been also using the moist air constants somewhere. To double check, I found [this derivation](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/00046973.1975.9648397).

This PR replaces eqs 11 and 12 from the [original paper](https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1029/97JD03735) and dry air `R` and `c_p` with eq 10 from [here](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/00046973.1975.9648397) using moist air `R` and `c_p`.

The results are basically unchanged. If you compare the example plot form the `AerosolActivation` documentation, the plot line shifts no more than its width. I still think that this is a more consistent approach.

This PR solves https://github.com/CliMA/CloudMicrophysics.jl/issues/28
title: Use moist air R and cp in aerosol activation
